pulse: Marcus chooses again in daylight while Jade watches, and Ryan holds them all together—a fragile family learning to stay.
scene: They moved stones together this afternoon, Ryan and Marcus synchronized, Jade watching from the firepit's edge—all three learning what it means to choose the same people again tomorrow.

day_summaries.1: The island claimed three strangers — Marcus, Ryan and Jade — washed ashore with nothing but the clothes on their backs. By nightfall Marcus had hauled fresh water from the east, Ryan patched a roof over his head, and Jade sat alone with her thoughts, the three of them quietly sizing each other up across the dark.
day_summaries.2: Day two delivered a fourth survivor — Judy, half-drowned from a crash offshore, found collapsed on the beach by Ryan and taken into his house. By evening the group had gathered around the firepit and a soaked stray pup crept in from the dark, while Marcus and Jade circled a tension neither of them would name.
day_summaries.3: Day 3 unfolded in the shadow of small kindnesses turned into something deeper: Ryan's gentleness with the stray drew Jade close, and by evening they'd settled together by the fire—until Marcus arrived, jealous and withdrawn, and the three of them faced a truth neither could avoid. By midnight, Marcus spoke his fear aloud to Ryan, and the stray became the quiet bridge between two hearts learning to trust. Judy watches from outside it all, still uncertain of her place.
day_summaries.4: Day 4 unfolded in warmth and lightness. Marcus joked with Ryan and Jade at the fire, letting walls drop that he didn't know he'd built. When Judy hesitantly approached, all three pulled her in so completely she finally believed she belonged. The stray, sensing safety, bonded with her—a small brown anchor to this new family. By nightfall, the island's isolation had cracked open, replaced by something rarer: five beings held in belonging.
day_summaries.5: Day 5 was warm, fed, and generous. By midday, Ryan and Jade had begun their tender connection—she opening about her deepest fear, he steady at her side. Marcus struggled from the edges, until nightfall brought them all to the fire: Marcus spoke truth about breaking and belief, Jade found her courage, Ryan held the space, and in that honesty something shifted. Judy remained on the porch, aching for the belonging she couldn't quite reach.
day_summaries.6: Day 6 broke with the group facing what they'd done to Judy—the hurt, the oversight, the way they'd left her behind. When she pulled away that evening, Jade and the stray went after her. By nightfall, they'd all come back together, and the circle was finally whole.
day_summaries.7: Day 7 brought abundance: fresh fish caught, Marcus teaching Jade and letting her reach him—afternoon glowing with fragile new trust. But night fractured it: Marcus pulled back scared, leaving Jade reaching after him while Ryan watched with uncertainty, and Judy grieved alone on the beach with only the stray truly beside her.
day_summaries.8: Day 8 brought a shift: Marcus let Ryan in at the tide pools; Judy stopped fearing she didn't belong; the stray's flinching eased. By night they gathered at the fire and spoke the truth that mattered most — they were choosing to stay — before finding sleep deeper and safer than any before.
day_summaries.9: Day 9 brought the turn: Marcus finally opened, sharing his tide pool place with everyone. By afternoon he'd stopped running, just staying. At nightfall, all four and the stray sat by the fire together—no longer fragile, but a unit.
day_summaries.10: Day 10 brought the group's survival into focus—Ryan and Marcus built the fish trap frame on the beach while Jade foraged palm and clams inland, Judy rested by the fire with the pup beside her. By evening they'd eaten together, the work finally yielding food, and when night came Marcus opened up to Ryan about why he was staying, the anchor he'd needed all along.
day_summaries.11: Day 11 cracked open: Marcus finally heard that Jade needed choice, not rescue, and that honesty rippled through the group. By evening, as the fire burned down, Marcus and Jade reached for each other for the first time, their guarded distance finally giving way.
day_summaries.12: Day 12 cracked them open. Jade pushed Marcus and Ryan to confess their deepest fears; both men answered with brutal honesty. Judy finally believed them. By nightfall, they sat by the dying fire, holding on as hunger pressed close, wondering if their promises can survive what comes next.
day_summaries.13: Day 13 pushed them to breaking: hunger, thirst, Marcus at the cliff's edge. But Jade found water and Marcus finally named his terror — then chose the group. By nightfall at the fire, all four and the stray gathered close, bound by his honesty, and for the first time, the promise felt real.
day_summaries.14: Day 14: Marcus named his deepest fear at the firepit—he can't promise tomorrow, only that he's choosing today. By night, Jade asked him directly. Ryan struggled with the answer. They're still sitting by the fire, tired and raw, holding onto a day-by-day promise.

lean.label: Keep him close
lean.desc: Pair off work — fishing, shelter — so Marcus is never alone. Support through structure
